findings.reachability: Reachable by any admin/privacy manager who triggers WordPress core's personal data erasure tool (wp_ajax_wp_privacy_erase_personal_data). The eraser callbacks are invoked by WP core which handles authentication.
findings.justification: Both erasers use `paged` (not offset-based iteration) to paginate through results, then delete records within the page. On the next page WP_Query computes OFFSET as (paged-1)*posts_per_page. Because previous-page records were deleted, remaining records have shifted to lower offsets, so page 2 skips them entirely and the eraser silently terminates with `done=true`, leaving up to N-1 batches of personal data undeleted — a GDPR-significant privacy compliance failure.

detection_pattern.section_name: Privacy Eraser: paged query with in-loop deletion causes incomplete erasure
detection_pattern.description: WordPress privacy erasers that use WP_Query's 'paged' argument while deleting records within the same loop will skip records on subsequent pages because deletions shift remaining rows to earlier offsets. Safe implementations use offset = (page-1)*per_page computed against a stable query (no deletions), or delete by fixed criteria each pass without relying on page offsets.
